opp.name: Mattel_S2P_May'26
opp.next_step: 6.10.26 KK fu again - if no response will push to bd 5.27.26 KK - FU with Maria on gartner follow up
opp.ais_why:
* Mattel is top-of-funnel: the only documented activity is a Gartner follow-up ("FU with Maria on gartner follow up"), so the relationship has not reached discovery.
* The opportunity scope is AI-heavy (Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA), so AI modules are part of the intended S2P footprint.
* Although AI-heavy modules are in scope (Merlin, Agentic AI, Merlin Intake, ANA), there is no customer-side signal of AI interest for this opportunity; Qualified at 10%.

sources: SF Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c all null
SF Next_Step__c references Gartner follow-up but no call or task detail
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI has been articulated. Salesforce fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, and Compelling_Event__c are all empty, and zero Avoma calls exist to surface what the buyer cares about. The rep's outreach mentions a Gartner follow-up (27 May Next Step), which implies a prior interaction or event, but no record of that conversation exists in Avoma or Salesforce tasks. Without a single buyer conversation we have no validated pain, no quantified impact, and no business case. The highest-leverage move is to secure a discovery call with Maria Gutierrez or another Mattel stakeholder and extract the operational pain, cost impact, and urgency that would justify a 120k S2P investment.

sources: SF Next_Step__c shows two unanswered follow-ups to Maria Gutierrez (27 May, 10 June)
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified. Zero OpportunityContactRole entries exist, so no one is formally mapped as Champion, and no Avoma calls exist to show who is advocating for Zycus internally. The rep has been following up with Maria Gutierrez (named in Next Step 27 May and 10 June), but Maria has not replied to either outreach, which raises the question of whether she is engaged, whether the contact info is correct, or whether she was ever a real champion. Salesforce field X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c is empty, and no call transcript shows anyone coaching us, opening doors, or driving the deal forward on our behalf. At this stage the move is to re-engage Maria (or find the right contact if she is not responsive) and assess whether she has the influence, access to the EB/DM, and willingness to champion Zycus. If she is not the champion, ask her who is.

sources: SF Next_Step__c mentions Gartner but no competitive context
competition.narrative: Competition is unknown. Salesforce fields Competitors__c and Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c are empty, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c (current stack) is null, and zero Avoma calls exist to reveal whether Mattel is benchmarking Zycus against Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Jaggaer, or an incumbent. The product scope is broad and includes Merlin Agentic AI, which is a differentiator, but without knowing the competitive set we cannot position our AI story or price strategically. The rep's Gartner reference (27 May Next Step) could imply a Gartner-led vendor shortlist, but no confirmation exists. The move is to ask Maria or the decision-maker on the re-engagement call who else they are evaluating, what their current S2P stack looks like, and whether AI capability is a formal decision criterion. That will tell us the wedge and how to win.

sources: SF Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Compelling_Event__c null
SF Next_Step__c references Gartner but no pain detail
identify_pain.narrative: No operational or financial pain has been confirmed. Salesforce fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, and Compelling_Event__c are all empty, and zero Avoma discovery or demo calls exist to tell us what is broken in their current S2P process. The broad product scope (iContract through Merlin Agentic AI) implies a full-suite need, which typically signals legacy-system replacement or greenfield build, but we have no confirmation. The rep's Gartner follow-up reference (27 May Next Step) hints at a prior touchpoint, possibly a Gartner inquiry or event, but the context is missing. Without confirmed pain we cannot build urgency, quantify ROI, or differentiate against alternatives. The move is to use the re-engagement call to ask what is not working today, what triggered this evaluation, and who owns the pain (procurement, finance, supply chain).

sources: No contracting or procurement fields populated
paper_process.narrative: Contracting and procurement mechanics are completely unknown. No Salesforce fields capture MSA/SOW status, legal review, InfoSec requirements, or SI/partner involvement, and zero Avoma calls exist to surface Mattel's contracting workflow. For a 120k S2P deal with ten modules, we should expect procurement-led contracting, possible MSA negotiation, and InfoSec/data-privacy review. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null, so we do not know if this is a replacement deal with a forcing function or a net-new build. The move is to ask on the re-engagement call whether Mattel has a preferred procurement channel, whether they require a new MSA or have one on file, and what the InfoSec/legal review process looks like.

sources: SF Next_Step__c names Maria Gutierrez but no title/role
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified. There are zero OpportunityContactRole entries, no EB-related Salesforce fields populated (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c are all empty), and no Avoma calls to surface who controls the commercial decision. The rep has been reaching out to Maria Gutierrez (named in Next Step 27 May and tasks), but her title, role, and budget authority are unknown because she is not in the contact-role map and no call transcript exists. At 120k ACV for a full S2P suite (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA), procurement or a VP/C-level sponsor will control approval. The move is to confirm Maria's role and ask her directly who approves the budget and procurement process, then map that person into the deal.

sources: SF Next_Step__c implies outreach but no process detail
decision_process.narrative: The buyer's decision process is unknown. Salesforce field What_is_the_decision_process__c is empty, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c and Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c are null, and zero Avoma calls exist to reveal whether this is an RFP, a sole-source negotiation, or a renewals discussion. We do not know the evaluation timeline, the number of vendors being considered, or the approval chain. The close date is May 2027 (320 days out), which is long enough for a full enterprise RFP cycle, but without a process map we cannot stage our moves or forecast the real close window. The move is to ask Maria or the actual decision-maker to walk us through their evaluation and approval steps, confirm the timeline, and identify gate reviews or committee decisions that pace the deal.

sources: Products__c lists ten modules but no supporting use-case or BRD detail
decision_criteria.narrative: No decision criteria have been documented. Salesforce fields Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, and AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c are empty, and zero Avoma calls or RFP/RFI artifacts exist to tell us what Mattel is evaluating or how they will score vendors. The product scope is broad (ten modules including Merlin Agentic AI), which suggests either a full-suite RFP or an upsell conversation, but we have no confirmation of which, no BRD, and no weighted criteria. Without criteria we cannot position our strengths or de-risk our gaps. The move is to use the first re-engagement call to ask what triggered this evaluation, what their top three decision factors are, and whether AI/Merlin capability is a formal requirement or a nice-to-have.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

Product scope includes four AI modules (Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA), but we do not know if that scope came from the buyer or was proposed by the rep. Tier is unassessable until we have a discovery call.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'At Risk' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 4 reflects a weak position: differentiation strength -0.50 (weight 20). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum is flat at 50: no forward or backward motion was observed.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity). Reduced because the most recent contact is stale (recency ×0.68).
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (4), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

deal_movement.summary: Deal was created 20 May 2026 in Qualified stage and has not progressed. Rep reached out 27 May and again 10 June with no buyer reply. Last Salesforce activity 10 June (25 days ago) reflects rep-side outreach, not buyer engagement. No stage changes, no amount changes, no close-date slips recorded in the 46-day life of the deal. The deal is effectively dormant pending buyer response.

items.detail: Deal is cold with zero buyer engagement. No matched Avoma calls, no OpportunityContactRole entries, and rep outreach 27 May and 10 Jun yielded no reply. Risk is the deal never progresses beyond this ghost state.

champion_strength.source: SF Next_Step__c, OpportunityContactRole query (zero entries), Avoma discovery (zero calls)
champion_strength.summary: No champion exists. Maria Gutierrez is the only named contact but has not replied to two follow-ups (27 May, 10 June), is not in the OpportunityContactRole map, and her title and role are unknown. Zero Avoma calls exist to show advocacy or access to the EB/DM. Without a champion the deal cannot progress.

items.action: Re-engage Maria Gutierrez by phone and email to confirm she is the right contact, validate the deal is real, and secure a discovery call to extract pain, decision process, and stakeholder map. If no reply within 7 days, escalate to the deal owner's manager to research alternate Mattel contacts (LinkedIn, Zycus network, partner channel) and pivot to a multi-threaded approach.
items.trigger: Two unanswered follow-ups to Maria Gutierrez (27 May: 'FU with Maria on gartner follow up'; 10 June: 'fu again - if no response will push to bd'). Zero Avoma calls, zero contact roles, and no buyer engagement in 46 days.
items.expected_effect: Confirms whether the deal is real, surfaces the right buyer contacts, and establishes two-way engagement. If successful, enables discovery and MEDDPICC build. If unsuccessful, surfaces the need to disqualify or pivot.

items.action: On the re-engagement call (or first successful buyer conversation), run a full discovery: ask what triggered this evaluation, what is broken in their current S2P process, who owns the pain (procurement, finance, supply chain), who approves the budget, what the decision process and timeline look like, and who else they are evaluating. Extract at least three specific pain points and one quantified impact metric (cost, cycle time, error rate). Document the EB, DM, and champion, and map them into OpportunityContactRole.
items.trigger: Zero MEDDPICC coverage: Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c, X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Business_Requirements__c, What_is_the_decision_process__c, X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c all null. Zero Avoma calls to extract discovery intel.
items.expected_effect: Builds MEDDPICC foundation (Metrics, Pain, EB, Decision Process, Champion), validates the deal is real, and enables stage progression to the relevant stakeholder or disqualification if the deal does not meet ICP.

items.action: On the discovery call, confirm whether Merlin Agentic AI and the four AI modules in scope (Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) are buyer-driven requirements or rep-proposed. If buyer-driven, ask what AI use cases they care about (negotiation automation, intake triage, contract intelligence, predictive analytics) and position Zycus Merlin as the differentiator. If rep-proposed, validate buyer AI appetite and adjust scope to match their actual priorities. Log the AI signal in AIS fields.
items.trigger: Product scope includes Merlin;iRisk;Merlin Intake;Agentic AI;ANA (four AI modules) but AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c are null and zero calls exist to confirm buyer AI appetite. Without AI validation we cannot position the differentiator or price the AI modules defensibly.
items.expected_effect: Confirms whether AI is a real decision criterion, enables AI-led differentiation if buyer is AI Hungry, and prevents over-scoping or pricing risk if buyer has no AI appetite.

items.action: Multi-thread the deal by identifying and engaging at least two additional Mattel stakeholders beyond Maria Gutierrez: the Economic Buyer (likely a VP Procurement, CFO, or C-level sponsor) and one influencer (procurement analyst, supply chain lead, or IT/systems owner). Use the discovery call to ask Maria for introductions, or research LinkedIn and the Zycus network to find the right contacts. Map all three into OpportunityContactRole.
items.trigger: Zero OpportunityContactRole entries. Only Maria Gutierrez named in Next Step, unresponsive to two follow-ups, role and title unknown. Single-threaded deals stall when the one contact disengages.
items.expected_effect: De-risks single-thread dependency, opens multiple buyer relationships, and increases the chance of finding the real decision-maker and champion.

items.action: After the discovery call, if the deal is validated as real, update Salesforce to reflect MEDDPICC progress: log the EB, DM, and champion in OpportunityContactRole, populate Customer_Business_Problem__c and Compelling_Event__c with the pain and urgency extracted, log the competitive set in Competitors__c, and document the decision process in What_is_the_decision_process__c. Move stage to the relevant stakeholder only when discovery is complete and MEDDPICC is at least partially covered.
items.trigger: All MEDDPICC fields null and zero contact roles. Hygiene gap prevents accurate forecasting and pipeline review.
items.expected_effect: Improves deal hygiene, enables accurate forecast, and provides visibility to leadership and the broader deal team.

north_star_verdict.math: 320 days to close (close date 2027-05-21). Created 20 May 2026, so 46 days old. Time in Qualified stage is approximately 46 days. No stage progression yet. LastActivityDate 10 June 2026 (25 days ago) reflects rep-side outreach per Next Step, not buyer engagement. No forward slip recorded. At this stage and distance, pace is not the constraint; the constraint is establishing two-way buyer contact. Zero calls, zero contact roles, and no reply to two follow-ups signals the deal may not be real or the contact is wrong.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal is early-stage (Qualified) with 320 days to close but has zero buyer engagement: no returned calls, no contact roles, no Avoma meetings, and rep outreach on 27 May and 10 June yielded no reply. MEDDPICC is a complete gap. The deal needs immediate buyer re-engagement to validate it is real.

flags: Zero buyer-side contact roles on the opportunity. Add the procurement stakeholders Mattel has engaged (or should engage) as OpportunityContactRole entries and multi-thread beyond a single point of contact to secure a champion and map the Economic Buyer.

No discovery call has occurred. The deal lacks every MEDDPICC element. Run a structured discovery session with Mattel procurement leadership to capture pain, quantified value case, evaluation criteria, competitive shortlist, decision process, and budget authority before investing further effort.
Zero buyer engagement: no Avoma calls, no OpportunityContactRole entries, and rep outreach 27 May and 10 June yielded no reply. Deal may not be real.
Single-threaded and contact unresponsive: only Maria Gutierrez named (Next Step), no reply to two follow-ups, no title or role confirmed, not in contact-role map.
No MEDDPICC coverage: Metrics, EB, Decision Criteria, Decision Process, Paper Process, Pain, Champion, and Competition are all gaps.
No stakeholder map: zero OpportunityContactRole entries, so no EB, no DM, no champion, no influencer network.
No AI positioning: AIS fields null, and product scope includes Merlin Agentic AI but no evidence buyer cares about AI or that we have positioned it.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal fails every enterprise-sales best practice. Zero buyer engagement (no calls, no replies to two follow-ups), single-threaded (only Maria Gutierrez named, unresponsive), no stakeholder map (zero contact roles), no MEDDPICC coverage (all eight elements are gaps), and no evidence the deal is real. At 46 days old and 320 days to close, this is not a pacing problem; it is a validation problem. The deal needs immediate re-engagement to confirm it exists.

No discovery call has occurred. The deal lacks every MEDDPICC element. Run a structured discovery session with Mattel procurement leadership to capture pain, quantified value case, evaluation criteria, competitive shortlist, decision process, and budget authority before investing further effort.

Close date 2027-05-21 is 329 days out but the deal is in early Qualified stage with no buyer engagement. If Mattel does not reply to re-engagement within 10 business days, recommend disqualifying or pushing the close date to a realistic future quarter and deprioritizing in favor of live deals.
